label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the given text and understand the context. The text is about how animals are adapted to cross roads safely using ecopassages. These are paths that are both over and under roads to help animals avoid road accidents. The text also mentions that these ecopassages are made to look like a natural part of the area by planting trees around them. The question is asking the reason why the writer is suggesting that visitors and drivers should look around when traveling. The possible answers are: A) wild animals may attack cars B) wild animals may jam the road C) they may see wild animals in the park D) they may see wild animals on ecopassages The last paragraph says, "The next time you visit a park or drive through an area with a lot of wildlife, look around. You might see an animals overpass!" The key part here is "You might see an animals overpass!" which means that the writer is suggesting that people look around because they might see wild animals using these ecopassages. Looking at the answer options, option D) they may see wild animals on ecopassages fits perfectly
